hadiths.chapter: 77. THE DISLIKE OF SLEEPING AFTER THE FAJR PRAYER
hadiths.en:
Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"Sleeping at the beginning of the day is wastefulness, the nap at midday is a blessing, 
sleeping after Asr (afternoon prayer) is foolishness, and sleeping between the two 
night prayers (Maghrib and Isha) deprives one of sustenance."  
[AL SADUQ] 
There are four types of sleep : the sleep of prophets is on their backs due to their 
communication with revelation, the sleep of believers is on their right sides, the sleep 
of disbelievers is on their left sides, and the sleep of devils is on their faces.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 502 • Al -Ash'athiyat, Part 1, p. 157 • Makarem al -
Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 288 • The Makarem al -Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 305 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 503 • Bihar Al -
Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 185 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 5, p. 113 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 77, H. 1442

hadiths.chapter: 77. THE DISLIKE OF SLEEPING AFTER THE FAJR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1444  -  وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ن َّوْم  مِن ْ ع َّي ْزِ سَّهَّزٍ وَّ ص َّحِك   مِن ْ ع َّي ْزِ عَّح َّث ٍ وَّ 
إ َّكْل  عَّلَّى إَّلس ِّن َّغِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"If you see someone sleeping on their face, wake them up."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 503 • Al-Ādāb al-Dīniyyah, Part 1, p. 127 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 
26, p. 542 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 503 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 77, H. 1443

hadiths.chapter: 77. THE DISLIKE OF SLEEPING AFTER THE FAJR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1445  -  وَّ إ َّي َّى إ َّعْزَّإن ِى    إ ِلَّى إَّلن  َّن ِى ِّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ق َّق َّالَّ ي َّا رَّسُّولَّ إَّلل َّهِ إ ِن ِّى  كُّن ْث ُّ ذ َّكُّورإً   وَّ إ ِن ِّى  صِزْب ُّ ن َّسِي  اً 
ق َّق َّال زَّج َّغَّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ ذ ِهْن ُّهُّ .
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "Three things bring forth the dislike (anger) of Allah {SWT}, the 
Majestic: sleeping without fatigue, laughing without wonder, and eating when already 
full”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 503 • Al-Khasal, Part 1, p. 89 • Al-Ādāb al-Dīniyyah, Part 
1, p. 127 • Aʿlām al-Dīn, Part 1, p. 152 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 26, p. 542 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 503 • Wasail Al-
Shia, Vol. 12, p. 115 • Bihar Al-Anwar, V. 63, p. 332 • Bihar Al Anwar, V. 73, p. 58 • Bihar Al Anwar, V. 73, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 77, H. 1444

hadiths.chapter: 77. THE DISLIKE OF SLEEPING AFTER THE FAJR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1446   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو ن َّصِي زٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال مٍ ي َّسْق ِكُّهُّ وَّ ذ ُّو إَّلْمَّالِ  
إَّلْكَّي ِي زِ لأَّ إ َّمِي ن َّ لَّهُّ وَّ إَّلْق َّان ِلُّ ق ِى  إَّلن  َّاسِ إَّلز  ُّورَّ وَّ إَّلْن ُّهْي َّان َّ عَّن ْ عَّزَّض ٍ مِ ن َّ إَّلذ ُّن ْي َّا ي َّن َّالُّهُّ وَّ إَّلْمَّا ْج ُّوذ ُّ ن ِالْمَّالِ إَّلْكَّي ِي زِ وَّ لأَّ مَّالَّ 
لَّهُّ وَّ إَّلْمُّحِث  ُّ جَّن ِي ن اً ي َّي َّوَّق  َّغُّ ق ِزَّإق َّهُّ .
hadiths.en:
A Bedouin came to the Prophet {saws} and said: "O’ Messenger of Allah {saws}, I 
used to have a strong memory but now I have become forgetful."  
The Prophet {saws} asked: "Did you use to take a midday nap (Qailulah)?"  
The man replied: "Yes."  
The Prophet {saws} then asked: "And did you stop doing that?"  
The man answered, "Yes."  
The Prophet {saws} said: "Resume it." The man did so and his memory returned. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 503 • Al-Ādāb al-Dīniyyah, Part 1, p. 127 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 
26, p. 542 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 501 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 77, H. 1445

hadiths.chapter: 77. THE DISLIKE OF SLEEPING AFTER THE FAJR PRAYER
hadiths.en:
Abu Basir narrated from Imam Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar Al -Sadiq {a.s} who 
said:  
"There are five types of people who cannot sleep: one who is anxious about shedding 
blood, the possessor of a great amount of wealth who lacks a trustworthy guardian, 
one who spreads lies and false accusations among people to gain a worldly benefit, 
the person burdened with a large amount of wealth but has none himself, and one who 
deeply loves someone and fears separation from them."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 503 • Al-Khisal, Part 1, p. 296 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 26, p. 545 
• Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 67, p. 15 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 179 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 77, H. 1446

hadiths.chapter: 77. THE DISLIKE OF SLEEPING AFTER THE FAJR PRAYER
hadiths.en:
It is narrated:  
"Take a midday nap (Qailulah), for indeed Allah {SWT} nourishes and provides drink to 
the fasting person during their sleep." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 4, p. 65 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 503 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-
Faqih, Vol. 2, p. 76 • Thawab al-A’mal wa Iqab al-A’mal, Part 1, p. 51 • The Virtues of the Three Months, Part 
1, p. 120 • Al-Ādāb al-Dīniyyah, Part 1, p. 127 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 29 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 26, p. 542 • Wasail Al-
Shia, Vol. 6, p. 501 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 10, p. 136 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 77, H. 1447

reference.location: Volume 1 › THE DISLIKE OF SLEEPING AFTER THE FAJR PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٧، ح ١٤٤٧

hadiths.chapter: 77. THE DISLIKE OF SLEEPING AFTER THE FAJR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1449  -  وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ:  «  
إ ِسْزَّإن ِي لَّ مَّا ن َّي ْن َّ طُّلُّوعِ إَّلْق َّح ْزِ إ ِلَّى طُّلُّوعِ إَّلس  َّمْسِ ق َّمَّن ْ ن َّامَّ ي ِلْك َّ إَّلس َّاعَّه َّ لَّمْ ي َّن ْز ِلْ ن َّصِي ن ُّهُّ ق كَّان َّ  إ ِذ َّإ إِن ْي َّن َّهَّ ق َّلاَّ ي َّزَّى 
ن َّصِي ن َّهُّ إِجْي َّاح َّ إ ِلَّى إَّلس ُّو َّإلِ وَّ إَّلط َّلَّث ِ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated,  
"Take a midday nap (Qailulah), for indeed the Shaytan does not take a nap." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 503 • Al-Ādāb al-Dīniyyah, Part 1, p. 127 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 
26, p. 542 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 501 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 77, H. 1448

hadiths.chapter: 77. THE DISLIKE OF SLEEPING AFTER THE FAJR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1450   -  وَّق َّال إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :   ق ِى  ق َّوْلِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل   ق َّال 
إ ذَّمَّ مَّا ن َّي ْن َّ طُّلُّوعِ إَّلْق َّح ْزِ إ ِلَّى طُّلُّوعِ إَّلس  َّمْسِ ق َّمَّن ْ ي َّن َّامُّ ق ِي مَّا ن َّي ْن َّهُّمَّا ي َّن َّامُّ عَّن ْ رِر ْق ِهِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said:  
"Sleeping in the morning (after dawn) is an omen of misfortune; it deprives one of 
sustenance and pales the complexion. The manna and quails used to descend upon 
the Children of Israel between the time of dawn until sunrise, so whoever slept during 
that ho ur would not receive their share. When they woke up and did not find their 
portion, they were compelled to ask and seek provision”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 77, H. 1449

reference.location: Volume 1 › THE DISLIKE OF SLEEPING AFTER THE FAJR PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٧، ح ١٤٤٩

hadiths.chapter: 77. THE DISLIKE OF SLEEPING AFTER THE FAJR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1451  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّعَّم َّزُّ ن ْن ج َّلا َّذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ: كَّان َّ وَّ هُّوَّ ن ِح ُّزَّإسَّان َّ إ ِذ َّإ صَّل َّى إَّلْق َّح ْزَّ  
ج َّلَّسَّ ق ِى  مُّصَّلا َّةُّ إ ِلَّى إ َّن ْ ي َّطْلُّغَّ إَّلس  َّمْسُّ ي ُّم َّ ي ُّو ْي َّى ن ِح َّزِي طَّه ٍ ق ِي هَّا مَّسَّاوِي ك ُّ ق َّي َّسْي َّاك ُّ ن ِهَّا وَّإجِذإً ن َّ عْذَّ وَّإجِ ذٍ ي ُّم َّ ي ُّو ْي َّى  
ن ِكُّن ْذُّرٍ ق َّي َّمْص َّع ُّهُّ ي ُّم َّ ي َّذَّعُّ ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّي ُّو ْي َّى ن ِالْمُّصْحَّف ِ ق َّي َّق ْزَّإ ُّ ق ِي هِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam al-Ridha {a.s} said regarding the verse of Allah {SWT}, the Exalted: "By those 
who distribute commands"  
He (Imam {a.s}) explained: "The angels distribute the sustenance of the children of 
Adam from the time of dawn until sunrise. Therefore, whoever sleeps during this 
period sleeps away their sustenance." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 77, H. 1450

hadiths.chapter: 77. THE DISLIKE OF SLEEPING AFTER THE FAJR PRAYER
hadiths.ar:
1452  -  وَّق َّال رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ :  «
إَّلل َّهُّ مِن َّ إَّلن  َّارِ .
hadiths.en:
Muammar ibn Khallad narrated from Imam al -Ridha {a.s} that when he was in 
Khurasan, after performing the Fajr prayer, he would sit in his prayer place until the 
sun rose. Then, a pouch containing miswaks (tooth-cleaning sticks) would be brought 
to him, and he would use them one by one. Afterward, he would be given frankincense, 
which he would chew for some time before leaving it aside. He would then be brought 
a Qur'an, which he would read from.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 504 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1554 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, 
p. 26 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 460 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 77, H. 1451
reference.chapter: THE DISLIKE OF SLEEPING AFTER THE FAJR PRAYER
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE DISLIKE OF SLEEPING AFTER THE FAJR PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٧، ح ١٤٥١

hadiths.chapter: 77. THE DISLIKE OF SLEEPING AFTER THE FAJR PRAYER
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said:  
"Whoever remains seated in their place of prayer after the Fajr prayer until the sun 
rises, Allah {SWT} will protect them from the Fire." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 504 • Tahdhib Al-
Ahkam, Part 2, p. 139 • Makarem al-Akhlaq, Part 1, p. 304 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1553 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, 
p. 459

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 77, H. 1452

reference.location: Volume 1 › THE DISLIKE OF SLEEPING AFTER THE FAJR PRAYER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٧، ح ١٤٥٢

hadiths.chapter: 78. THE PRAYER OF THE TWO EIDS
hadiths.ar:
1454   -  عَّن ْ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال لَّهُّمَّا وَّ لأَّ  ن َّعْذَّهُّمَّا  
صَّلاَّة   ذ َّلِك َّ إَّلْي َّوْمَّ إ ِلَّى إَّلز  َّوَّإلِ . 
ووج وب  إلعي ذ إ ن ما هو مغ إ مام عذل .
hadiths.en:
Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} that 
He said: "The prayers of the two Eids (Eid al -Fitr and Eid al -Adha) are obligatory, and 
the prayer of the eclipse (Salat al-Kusoof) is also obligatory." 
[AL SADUQ] 
It means that they are among the minor obligatory acts, and the minor obligations are 
considered Sunnahs, according to the narration of Hariz.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 504 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 443 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1286 
• Wasail Al-Shia, Part 7, p. 419 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 7, p. 420 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 7, p. 483 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 78, H. 1453
reference.chapter: THE PRAYER OF THE TWO EIDS
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE PRAYER OF THE TWO EIDS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٨، ح ١٤٥٣

hadiths.chapter: 78. THE PRAYER OF THE TWO EIDS
hadiths.en:
From Zurarah, from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} who said:  
"The prayer of the two Eids (Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha) with the Imam is a Sunnah, and 
there is no prayer before or after them on that day until noon (zawal)."  
[AL SADUQ] 
The obligation of the holiday is only with a just Imam.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 506 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 3, p. 134 • Al -Istibsar, 
Part 1, p. 443 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1290 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 7, p. 419  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 78, H. 1454
reference.chapter: THE PRAYER OF THE TWO EIDS
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE PRAYER OF THE TWO EIDS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٨، ح ١٤٥٤

hadiths.chapter: 78. THE PRAYER OF THE TWO EIDS
hadiths.ar:
1456  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّعْي َّن َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال ى إ ِلأ َّ مَّغَّ إ ِمَّامٍ 
عَّاذِلٍ .
hadiths.en:
Sama’a bin Mehran narrated from Imam Jafar Al -Sadiq {a.s} who said:  
"There is no prayer on the two Eids (Eid al -Fitr and Eid al -Adha) except with an Imam, 
but if you pray alone, there is no issue." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 506 • Thawab al-A’mal wa Iqab al-A’mal, Part 1, p. 78 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 128 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 445 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1288 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 
7, p. 421 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 87, p. 354 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 78, H. 1455
reference.chapter: THE PRAYER OF THE TWO EIDS
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE PRAYER OF THE TWO EIDS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٨، ح ١٤٥٥

hadiths.chapter: 78. THE PRAYER OF THE TWO EIDS
hadiths.ar:
1457 - وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إَّلص َّاذِق عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ْ صَّلاَّة ِ إَّلْأ َّص ْحَّى وَّ إَّلْق ِطْزِ ق َّق َّال 
ج َّمَّاعَّه ٍ وَّ كَّن ِّزْ سَّن ْعاً وَّ ج َّمْساً .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah bin A'yan narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} who 
said:  
"There is no prayer on the day of Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha except with a just Imam”. 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 459 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 506 • Thawab al -A’mal wa 
Iqab al-A’mal, Part 1, p. 78 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 128 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 444 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 
1286 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 7, p. 421 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 7, p. 421 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 7, p. 423 • Bihar 
Al-Anwar, Vol. 87, p. 354 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 78, H. 1456

hadiths.chapter: 78. THE PRAYER OF THE TWO EIDS
hadiths.ar:
1458  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مَّن ْصُّورُّ ن ْن ُّ جَّار ِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال مَّ إَّلْأ َّص ْحَّى  
ق َّصَّل َّى ق ِى  ن َّي ْي ِهِ رَّكْعَّي َّي ْن ِ ي ُّم َّ ص َّح َّى .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} was asked about the prayers of Eid 
al-Adha and Eid al-Fitr, and He said:  
"Pray them as two units (rak'ahs), either in congregation or individually, and say seven 
takbirs (Allahu Akbar) in the first rak'ah and five in the second”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 78, H. 1457
reference.chapter: THE PRAYER OF THE TWO EIDS
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE PRAYER OF THE TWO EIDS

hadiths.chapter: 78. THE PRAYER OF THE TWO EIDS
hadiths.ar:
1459  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ج َّعْق َّزُّ ن ْن ُّ ن َّس ِي زٍ عَّن ْ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ سِن َّان ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال   لَّمْ ي َّس ْهَّذْ ج َّمَّاعَّه َّ 
إَّلن  َّاسِ ق ِى  إَّلْعِي ذَّي ْن ِ ق َّلْي َّع ْي َّسِلْ وَّ لْي َّي َّطَّي  َّث ْ ن ِمَّا وَّج َّذَّ وَّ ي ُّصَّلِّى  ق ِى  ن َّي ْي ِهِ وَّجْذَّةُّ  كَّمَّا ي ُّصَّلِّى  ق ِى  ج َّمَّاعَّه ٍ .
hadiths.en:
Mansur ibn Hazim narrated from Imam Jafar Al -Sadiq {a.s} who said:  
"My father {a.s} fell ill on the day of Eid al -Adha, so he prayed two units (rak'ahs) in his 
home and then offered the sacrifice." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 507 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 3, p. 136 • Tahdhib Al -
Ahkam, Part 3, p. 288 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 445 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1291 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 7, p. 425 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 78, H. 1458

hadiths.chapter: 78. THE PRAYER OF THE TWO EIDS
hadiths.ar:
1460  -  وَّ رَّوَّى هَّارُّون ُّ ن ْن ُّ جَّمْز َّة َّ إَّلْع َّن َّوِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال ق ِطْزِ وَّ إَّلْأ َّص ْحَّى إ ِلَّى  
إَّلْح َّن  َّان َّه ِ جَّسَّن   لِمَّن ِ إِسْي َّطَّاعَّ  إَّلْح ُّزُّوح َّ إ ِلَّي ْهَّا 
ن َّي ْي ِهِ ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Ja'far ibn Bashir narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan from Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} who said:  
"Whoever does not attend the congregation of people for the Eid prayers should 
perform the ritual bath (ghusl), apply whatever perfume is available, and then pray at 
home alone as he would pray in congregation”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 507 • Al-Muqni’ah, Part 1, p. 202 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 
444 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 7, p. 424 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 87, p. 355 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 87, p. 372 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 78, H. 1459

hadiths.chapter: 78. THE PRAYER OF THE TWO EIDS
hadiths.ar: 1461  - وَّ رَّوَّى إِن ْن ُّ إَّلْمُّع ِي زَّة ِ عَّن ِ إَّلْق َّاسِمِ ن ْن ِ إَّلْوَّلِي ذِ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ْ ع ُّسْلِ إَّلْأ َّص ْحَّى، ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Harun ibn Hamzah al-Ghanawi narrates from Imam Jafar Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Going out on the day of Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha to the open area (for prayer) is good 
for whoever is able to go."  
Harun asked: "What if a person is sick and unable to go out, should he pray at home?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "No."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 507 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 3, p. 288 • Al -Istibsar, 
Part 1, p. 445 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 9, p. 1294 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 7, p. 422 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 78, H. 1460

hadiths.chapter: 78. THE PRAYER OF THE TWO EIDS
hadiths.en:
Ibn al-Mughira narrated from al-Qasim ibn al-Walid, who said:  
I asked Imam {a.s} about the Ghusl (ritual purification bath) of Eid al-Adha.  
Imam {a.s} said: "It is obligatory except for those in Mina."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 507 • Iqbal Al-A’mal, Part 1, p. 423 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 
380 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 330 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 95, p. 294 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 78, H. 1461
